topic: Windsurf Wave 13 Guide: Arena Mode, Plan Mode and SWE-1.5

key_points: Wave 13 (Shipmas Edition) released December 24, 2025
SWE-1.5 free for 3 months and becomes default model
True parallel agents with Git Worktrees (up to 5 simultaneous sessions)
Dedicated zsh terminal with .zshrc support
Multi-pane and multi-tab Cascade interface
Wave 14 adds Arena Mode: blind head-to-head AI model comparisons
Plan Mode introduced for structured task planning before code execution
Arena Mode uses Elo rating system (like chess rankings)
Battle Groups: Frontier Arena, Fast Arena, Hybrid Arena
Plan Mode has a 'megaplan' shortcut for extra-thorough planning
Arena Mode free for one week after launch
SWE-1.5 delivers 40.08% on SWE-Bench-Pro
950 tokens/second — 13-14x faster than Claude Sonnet
Free SWE-1.5 for 3 months dramatically lowers cost barrier
Parallel worktree agents enable concurrent multi-file feature development
Windsurf positions as strongest Cursor alternative
Arena Mode runs two Cascade agents in parallel on the same prompt
Model identities are hidden during evaluation (blind test)
Votes feed into a personal and global Elo leaderboard
Up to 5 models can be selected in Arena Mode
Credits used based on selected models
Wave 13 upgrade enables enterprise MDM policy deployment
System-level rules and workflows via MDM for enterprise teams
Cascade terminal beta uses interactive zsh for better reliability
Parallel agents work on different branches without conflicts

stats.fact: SWE-1.5 achieves 40.08% accuracy on SWE-Bench-Pro, matching Claude Sonnet 3.5-level performance on real GitHub bug fixes
SWE-1.5 runs at ~950 tokens per second — 13-14x faster than Claude Sonnet 4.5
Wave 13 allows developers to run up to 5 parallel Cascade agents simultaneously in isolated Git worktrees
Windsurf Wave 14 with Arena Mode and Plan Mode released April/May 2026
Devin Local agent (introduced after Wave 14) is up to 30% more token-efficient than existing Cascade agent
Windsurf Pro pricing changed from $15/month credit-based to $20/month flat quota in March 2026
Arena Mode leaderboard (May 2026 top models): Frontier — Opus 4.6, Opus 4.5, Sonnet 4.5; Fast — SWE-1.5, Haiku 4.5, Gemini 3 Flash Low

angles: Wave 13 + Wave 14 together represent the biggest Windsurf leap: parallel agents, free frontier model, and blind model benchmarking
Arena Mode changes how developers choose AI models — real-world voting beats synthetic benchmarks
Plan Mode fills the 'thinking before coding' gap that pure autocomplete agents miss
SWE-1.5 as free default democratizes frontier-level AI coding for hobbyists and small teams
Git worktree-based parallel agents is the first commercial IDE feature that truly enables multi-task autonomous coding
Windsurf's breadth (40+ IDE plugins) vs Cursor's depth (VS Code only) is the defining strategic divide in AI IDEs
outline: H2: What Is Windsurf Wave 13? (Shipmas Edition Overview)
H2: SWE-1.5: Windsurf's Free Frontier Coding Model
H2: True Parallel Agents with Git Worktrees
H2: Wave 14 Continuation: Arena Mode and Plan Mode
H2: Arena Mode — Blind Head-to-Head Model Comparison
H2: Plan Mode — Think Before You Code
H2: Windsurf vs Cursor in 2026: How Wave 13/14 Changes the Race
H2: How to Get Started with All Wave 13 and Wave 14 Features
H2: Should You Switch to Windsurf in 2026?
